name: Herbie Fields
abstract: Herbie Fields (Herbert Bernfeld, May 24, 1919 – September 17, 1958) was an American jazz musician. He attended New York's famed Juilliard School of Music (1936–38) and served in the U.S.

works.t: Lady, be Good!: Oh, Lady, Be Good!

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Wilder Part 1
about_quotes.evidence: I played in another place called ... Bop City with Herbie Fields’ band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Wilder Part 1 interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: I went with Herbie Fields. Small band. Went with Herbie Fields and then I played around Philadelphia and New York...

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: one saxophone player that I remember, I was working with him when I went into the army -- before, just before I went into the army. His name was Herbie Fields.

about_quotes.speaker: George Handy
about_quotes.evidence: We got together and we decided to create a band for Herbie to front. I spent some time with that, we developed a book and we auditioned for a few hotels

about_quotes.speaker: Manny Albam
about_quotes.evidence: I wrote a chart for him on “Ghost of a Chance.” ... And I wrote a lot for him in the later 40s after the war.
about_quotes.doc_title: Manny Albam interviewed by Monk Rowe, Tarrytown, New York, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Arnett Cobb
about_quotes.evidence: He was my roommate in theaters. Gladys would assign he and I to dressing rooms together wherever we go.
